target.title: fix: WebChat duplicates external reply events
target.body:
Related: #85771

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users viewing WebChat could see the same assistant reply rendered twice when an external or plugin-returned reply arrived through duplicate live chat event delivery.

The observed failure was frontend-only: the affected WeChat/AKK session transcript contained a single assistant message, but WebChat rendered two identical assistant bubbles for the same reply.

## Why This Change Was Made

WebChat now treats gateway chat frames as idempotent by tracking recently accepted frame identities per UI state. The dedupe key uses the gateway event identity (`sessionKey`, `agentId`, `runId`, `seq`, and terminal/delta state), not message text, so legitimate repeated assistant text from later turns still renders normally.

This keeps the fix at the Control UI event-consumption boundary and avoids changing transcript persistence, plugin behavior, or gateway delivery semantics.

## User Impact

WebChat no longer displays duplicate assistant bubbles when the same live chat event reaches the client more than once. Normal repeated replies remain visible when they come from distinct turns or distinct event frames.

## Evidence

Focused regression coverage:

```text
pnpm test ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       131 passed (131)
```

Patch sanity:

```text
git diff --check -- ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts
```

Local structured review:

```text
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local

autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
overall: patch is correct (0.84)
```

Manual source/log evidence:

- The affected WeChat session transcript tail did not contain adjacent duplicate assistant messages, so the duplicate was isolated to WebChat rendering/live event consumption.
- Gateway `broadcastChatFinal` emits ordered chat frames with `runId` and `seq`; WebChat previously passed every received `chat` event directly into `handleChatEvent` without a frame-level idempotency guard.
target.author: scotthuang
